bibliographic_data.title: Semiparametric conformal prediction
bibliographic_data.abstract: Many risk-sensitive applications require well-calibrated prediction sets over multiple, potentially correlated target variables. In this work, we aim to construct the conformal prediction set accounting for the joint correlation structure of the vector-valued non-conformity scores. Drawing from the rich literature on multivariate quantiles and semiparametric statistics, we propose an algorithm to estimate the 1 −α quantile of the scores, where α is the user-specified miscoverage rate. In particular, we flexibly estimate the joint cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the scores using nonparametric vine copulas and improve the asymptotic efficiency of the quantile estimate using its influence function. The vine decomposition allows our method to scale well to a large number of targets. As well as guaranteeing asymptotically exact coverage, our method yields desired coverage and competitive efficiency on a range of real-world regression problems, including those with missing-at-random labels in the calibration set.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes an algorithm to estimate the 1 −α quantile of vector-valued non-conformity scores using nonparametric vine copulas, which improves the asymptotic efficiency and scalability for multi-target regression problems.
core_content.key_methodology: The method uses nonparametric vine copulas to flexibly estimate the joint cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the scores. The influence function is used to improve the quantile estimate's efficiency.
outcomes: Guarantees asymptotically exact coverage for conformal prediction sets
Yields desired coverage and competitive efficiency on real-world regression problems with missing-at-random labels
core_content.full_text_markdown: This paper addresses the challenge of constructing well-calibrated prediction sets in multi-target regression, where targets may be correlated. The authors propose a method that uses nonparametric vine copulas to estimate the 1 −α quantile of vector-valued non-conformity scores, ensuring asymptotically exact coverage while improving efficiency through the use of influence functions. This approach is demonstrated on real-world problems with missing-at-random labels in the calibration set.
